entry.title: Role assignments via SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Introduction to Identity Provisioning service

SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning (IPS) is a core service of SAP Business Technology Platform. It is used to provision identities and their authorizations between source and target systems.

The source system is the system that stores the user information for example SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, etc. The target system is the system where this information is consumed / sent to, in this case SAP Build Work Zone.  Identity Provisioning is a central service that can be used to connect a variety of SAP cloud/on-premise and other selected 3rd party systems.

Integration of SAP Build Work Zone with Identity Authentication (IAS)

It is recommended to set up the SAP BTP platform-level trust and it is required to have OIDC-based trust on the subaccount-level established using the 'establish trust' feature. This allows applications like SAP Build Work Zone to integrate directly with IAS. If other trusted Identity Providers (IdPs) are configured on the subaccount level, temporarily disable them during the initial setup.

Once you have the above prerequisites ready, configuring the direct integration with IAS is done using a few simple steps. An administrator can do this easily through the Work Zone's Design Time UI from the Site Manager > Settings > Identity Authentication screen. As shown in the screenshot below.

After the setup has been completed successfully, the below screes will show meaning that the dedicated application in IAS will be created.

If you want to learn more about the integration of SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Cloud Identity services and different trust levels, please head over to this blogpost. 

Exploring Role Assignments in Identity Provisioning service

Connecting to content providers is a key integration mechanism to make different business apps available to users in SAP Build Work Zone. For the configuration of content provider, the role assignment is a core aspect. There are two options available for assigning those content provider roles inside SAP Build Work Zone:

  1. Roles are automatically created as role collections on the SAP BTP subaccount following a specific syntax or prefix. The role assignment is done afterwards.
  2. Roles are directly assigned inside SAP Build Work Zone, using a dedicated API. This presents one of two REST APIs based on the System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM 2.0) specification. The SCIM API is used to create a base SCIM user record alongside the SCIM groups representing the required roles from the source system. The default for using this API is the IPS with a dedicated connector available for these role assignments. As an alternative, the API can be connected to from any other external client.

What is new?

Administrators can now explore the role assignments that are provisioned by the Identity Provisioning service. The prerequisite is that you have configured the integration with the Identity Provisioning service and the status of the connector is Connected. It is available is all SAP Build Work Zone editions including SAP Start, SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone.

This new capability is available in the site manager under Settings > Identity Provisioning screen as shown below.

This enhancement allows the administrator to explore all the roles that were assigned to specific users and provisioned by the Identity Provisioning service. The roles returned by the search include the content roles, which are also available in the Content Manager table, as well as assigned Derived roles and Composite roles which are not visible in the Content Manager table.

To search for users, one can either use email address or Global User ID to see role assignments. The table of role assignments contains the information as stated below.

Role ID: This is the role that is directly assigned to the content for example an app, group or catalog and to the site. The same role ID is also visible in the Content Manager table. To be able to view content in the site, the user must be assigned to this role directly or indirectly via derived/composite roles.

Composite/Derived role ID: Derived and Composite roles can be assigned to ABAP applications by the content provider. These roles are not visible in the Content Manager table. Composite roles combine several single roles into one menu. When a user is assigned to a composite role, they're indirectly assigned to multiple single roles. Derived roles are single roles that have inherited authorization characteristics from a “master” parent role.

Provider ID: The system ID of the remote content provider.

Provisioned on: The date when the role was first provisioned to SAP Build Work Zone. Note that this date doesn’t change when the role is updated.

Value proposition for SAP Build Work Zone administrators

This new enhancement of exploring role assignments provisioned by the Identity Provisioning service offers administrators a highly effective and user-friendly method for troubleshooting. It allows administrators to see what roles a user has been assigned to. The filter helps them explore the data that was previously hidden behind the scenes. This feature enables our Work Zone administrators to manage data more efficiently.

Learn more!

  • Check out the roadmap for more information on new product features
  • Learn more about SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com & SAP Community
  • Learning Journey and Certification: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
  • Discovery Center missions: standard & advanced edition
  • Reference architectures: Cloud leading Identity Lifecycle / Cloud leading Identity Lifecycle Authorizations

entry.title: SAP Build 的新增功能 – 2024 年第 2 季度版本
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition，原文作者Jesse Sukhbayar。

开发者们，你们好！

随着我们将 SAP Build产品更紧密地整合在了一起，我们很高兴地宣布，现在我们的所有新功能都将整合到一个概览的博客中。SAP Build是一套全面的低代码和专业代码解决方案，旨在利用生成式 AI 加速应用开发和自动化。下面，我们来看看 SAP Build 2024 年第 2 季度的最新功能亮点！

增强 SAP Build 的集成：为了提高产品之间的互操作性，我们推出了 SAP Build Apps 与 SAP Build Process Automation 之间的集成功能。该增强可简化项目间部件的可维护性和高效重用。现在，无需手动 API 配置即可无缝探索和重用已发布的流程。

下面列出了产品特定的更新：

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

导出后端数据：对于后端项目，现在可以将可视云功能数据导出到 Excel 文件。这使您可以轻松编辑和定制数据，并将其重新导入到数据浏览器中。今年早些时候，我们还为可视化云功能引入了角色和访问管理，从而增强了我们的内置治理功能。

 

默认情况下启用 BTP 身份验证：又一个适用于 SAP BTP 身份验证的更新。在此之前，通常需要从“权限”选项卡启用验证 - 我们移除了此步骤，因此现在默认情况下会为所有新应用程序启用 BTP 验证。这允许您充分利用 BTP 集成并从其他 SAP 系统访问数据。 今年还发布了移动身份验证，进一步改善了移动应用程序的企业就绪度。

  • 详细了解 SAP Build Apps 路线图的最新动态。

SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone（高级版）

工作区的创建流程和成员处理：在工作区的一组新功能中，您将找到新的工作区创建对话框、取消将外部工作区作为单独的工作区类型、提供了私有和公共工作区之间的一致用户体验以及扩展的访问控制概念。有关详细信息，请参阅此博客帖子。

 

统一的访问控制设置：访问控制设置已在所有工作区屏幕中对齐，并提供相同的访问选项。

支持将卡片作为应用可视化类型：现在支持将卡片作为本地应用的可视化类型，并在内容管理器（应用编辑器）可视化标签中进行维护。

基于角色的卡过滤：随着内容管理器过渡到作为卡管理工具后，所有卡都将分配给角色，并且将在运行时根据用户角色进行访问。

 

迁移现有独立卡：客户之前上传到管理控制台的卡将迁移到内容管理器。这些卡将在本地应用实现可视化，并将分配给所有人角色。

使用卡可视化直接部署应用：现在支持将包含卡可视化的应用程序直接从 SAP Business Application Studio 的 SAP Build Work Zone 开发工具扩展部署到子账户。

知识库集成：与 SAP Service Cloud Version 2 中的原生知识库小组件集成可用，并且可以进行相应配置。

SAP Start

集成更多云解决方案：SAP Fieldglass 将加入与 SAP Start 集成的可用 SAP 云解决方案列表，并且可以使用新的洞察卡。有关详细信息，请查看由 Kerstin Ott 撰写的此博客帖子。

 

SAP Task Center

支持第三方任务提供商：现在可以将第三方任务提供商连接到 SAP Task Center。这是客户和合作伙伴最期待的功能之一。更多信息，请参见 连接第三方任务提供商和 SAP Task Center 。

已启用公共视图：您现在可以访问关键用户预定义的公共视图。有关详细信息，请参阅使用视图和为终端用户创建公共视图。

支持新操作“申请、发布和转发”：申请、发布和转发现在可用于 SAP S/4HANA、SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公有云版本）和 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（私有云版本）中的任务。

有关 SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center 的详细信息：

  • 查看 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版和高级版和 SAP Start/SAP Task Center 的路线图
  • 以及 标准版/高级版本/SAP Start/SAP Task Center 的新功能查看器

SAP Build Process Automation

 

从 SAP Intelligent RPA 轻松迁移到 SAP Build Process Automation：现在可以更快、更轻松地从 SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (iRPA) 迁移到 SAP Build Process Automation。SAP Intelligent RPA 中新的产品内迁移功能通过将现有 iRPA 环境转换为 SAP Build Process Automation 来简化升级流程。不需要更复杂的导入或导出。它将机器人、工作流和其他自动化资产升级到 SAP Build Process Automation。您将受益于集成的无代码/低代码环境，从而加速流程自动化并提高生产力。有关如何迁移的详细指南，请访问 SAP 产品经理 @Alex-Fortin 的 SAP Community 博客帖子或我们的迁移指南。

 

 

 

与 SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM高级事件网格)的集成也已推出，提供超出基本集成之外的功能，以及强大的事件管理功能。  SAP 为事件驱动集成提供了两种解决方案：SAP Integration Suite、Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) 和 SAP Event Mesh。虽然两者都利用事件代理进行集成，但 AEM 凭借以下几个不同的优势脱颖而出：

  • 强大的事件管理功能可确保数据完整性、合规性和生命周期管理。
  • 提供跨各种公共云（AWS、Azure、GCP）、企业预置(on-premises)或边缘的灵活部署选项。
  • 能够处理大量的有效负载和大量的存储需求，使其成为复杂事件驱动架构的理想选择。
  • 全面管理事件的整个生命周期，包括设计、发布、发现和重放消息，以便排除故障或开发新应用程序。

有关如何设置 的详细信息，请参阅文档与高级事件网格（Advanced Event Mesh) 连接。

了解有关 SAP Build Process Automation 的更多信息：

  • 有关更多更新和改进，请参阅 What's New Viewer（新增功能查看器）。
  • 有关更多即将推出的功能和重点区域，请参阅 SAP Road Map Explorer。

SAP Build Code

SAP Build Code全面推出 + SAP S/4HANA 演示：SAP Build Code 于 3 月全面推出，我们很高兴看到许多开发人员正在测试并分享他们的反馈。如果您想将 SAP Business Application Studio 实例升级到 SAP Build Code，请查看产品经理 Rupa Maity 的博客。您还可以在此演示中了解 SAP Build Code 如何集成 S/4HANA Cloud 中的数据。

SAP Fiori 工具增强：SAP Build Code 的第二季度更新中对 SAP Fiori 工具进行了多项增强。最新版本在页面编辑器中引入了gen AI 功能、语义按钮等。有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP Fiori 工具发布博客。

 

SAPUI5 调整项目：SAP Business Application Studio 现在支持扩展 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）- 使用 SAPUI5 调整项目的重要功能。这允许您在无需更改原始应用程序的情况下，创建满足特定业务需求的应用程序变式。现在还可以在 SAP BTP ABAP 环境中使用 SAPUI5 调整项目。有关详细信息，请参阅 Help Portal。

MDK 的改进：最后，移动开发工具包 (MDK) 在其最新版本中引入了多项改进，包括新的 Fiori 样式工具栏、标签上方的附加控件等。请参阅 MDK 客户端 24.4 中的新增功能。

  • 了解有关 SAP Build Code 社区的更多信息。

👍喜欢阅读有关更新的信息吗？您对哪种增强功能跃跃欲试？通过评论与我们分享!

请注意，此博文介绍了最新功能和增强功能的亮点。

  • 有关所有更新的完整列表，请参阅 Help Portal SAP Build 的新增功能
  • 加入 SAP Builders 小组，获取产品资源和在社区提出问题。
  • 订阅我们的电子报，通过电子邮件获取SAP Build 的最新消息。

entry.title: Unleashing Centralized Digital Experience for Suppliers – SAP Build Workzone
entry.content:
A Wave of Modern Digital Workplace

In today's business landscape, digitalization and innovation pose significant challenges for companies. To meet the ever-evolving needs of customers, businesses must swiftly adopt cloud solutions. However, progress is often hindered by limited resources and the complexities of maintaining IT infrastructures with multiple applications. To overcome these challenges, companies need to adopt digital processes, reduce technological obstacles, and empower both experts and citizen developers to drive innovation. 

This blog post will explore how SAP Build Workzone empowers companies to unleash the full potential of their central digital workplace, creating a more connected and efficient ecosystem for suppliers.


Background & Business Challenges

Customer is committed to becoming an Intelligent Enterprise by leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). To achieve this, they are dedicated to modernizing and replacing outdated business processes, empowering IT professionals and business users with tools to efficiently create engaging business sites for employees, partners, suppliers, and customers. A suitable replacement for the current landscape of supplier resources and applications is imperative. The scattered information throughout the enterprise lacks a centralized access point, causing inefficiencies in navigating through numerous disconnected systems. Decentralized content creation further hinders the development of flexible business sites and pages. The customer’s objective is to boost user productivity and engagement through interactive workspaces, forums, and knowledge-based articles. By centralizing information and facilitating efficient content creation, the company aims to create a seamless and productive environment for all users.

 

Customer's Vision to Elevate Supplier Engagement

The vision for the Supplier Portal is the ultimate centralized platform for Vendors/Suppliers and external partners. The aim is to provide easy access to all necessary resources in one location, including the Supplier Handbook, Supplier Resources, Proxied Applications, Contingent Worker Resources, and the option to access the Restricted Supplier Portal content. The customer worked with the SAP MaxAttention team to create a Digital Workplace that is agile and consistent with SAP's user experience. In this process, the team incorporated the latest innovations of BTP to provide a better user experience and easier content management. After a successful proof-of-concept, the team confidently deployed the updated content for productive usage through internal and external user onboarding processes

 

SAP MaxAttention Engagement

The success of this project is a true testament to SAP MaxAttention’s expertise and commitment to delivering exceptional results. During this engagement, SAP MaxAttention team worked along with SAP Build Work Zone Product Development and the customer’s CoE teams. This collaboration resulted in quick ROI and increased value by maximizing existing SAP solutions, specifically in the space of SAP Build subscription. SAP Build is powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) which enables business experts to easily create and augment enterprise applications, automate tedious processes, and design engaging business sites by using visual tools and a rich set of services and pre-built content. SAP Build capabilities include:

  • SAP Build Code – SAP Build Code provides generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development. It offers a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management.
  • SAP Build Apps – Evolution of AppGyver to build mobile and web applications with a pixel-perfect design using the drag-and-drop functionality and a rich component library.
  • SAP Build Process Automation – Offers task and process automation leveraging robotic process automation (RPA) bots, workflows, forms, embedded artificial intelligence (AI), business rules, and decisions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone – Allows the creation of enterprise-grade custom business sites using new capabilities such as a flexible page builder and context-aware integrated UI cards.

Architecture

Below is the architecture of the digital Supplier Portal in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

Delivery Scope

Setup of SAP Build Work Zone

For the platform preparation, we started with onboarding activities in SAP BTP, for example, Sub-account setup, setting up SAML Trust with PingIDP for authentication, user management & provisioning, and authorization management, data privacy, workspace setup to configure areas and workspace.

Building a Central Entry Point (Home Page)

The company's home page has been redesigned and now serves as the landing page for the website, featuring protected content. The customer team has been given the authority to distinguish between the administrative aspects of the SAP Build Work Zone landing page and to use pre-built templates and layouts to implement best practices. The objective is to differentiate between the usage of different widgets and to decide whether to build a Workspace or leave the content on the Home Page.

Supplier Handbook

Supplier Handbook workspace hosts a collection of key resources and assets (e.g., revision-controlled documents) used by customer’s Vendors/Suppliers. Private workspace access is only for onboard suppliers. Some of the key requirements were to have an intuitive user experience, manage contents and their versions, and send communication to the stakeholders whenever changes are made to the contents. 

Supplier Resources

Supplier Resources is a public workspace that hosts a collection of resources and assets needed for supplier onboarding. The customer’s prospective and established suppliers can find all the publicly available documents, process documentation, and answers to frequently asked questions related to the customer’s Account Payable, Invoicing Process, etc. This workspace also provides an entry point to the Supplier Handbook that provides access to protected resources.

Just-In-Time Reports Integration via SAP Build Process Automation

One of the requirements of the project is to enable vendors to view stocking reports in real-time. To achieve this, we leveraged SAP Build Process Automation.  SAP Build Process Automation is a citizen developer solution to adapt, improve, and innovate business processes with no-code workflow management and robotic process automation capabilities.
Using this tool, we developed unattended BOT to publish reports from BI and S/4HANA. On a periodical basis, automatic synch between the FTP and the SAP Build Work Zone is triggered. This business requirement was prototyped using SAP Build Work Zone OData API. The below diagrams illustrate the technical architecture of the solution.  

Supplier Registration Forms

To fulfill the need for supplier diversity, the existing registration form for suppliers to request access to a private workspace contains various form controls. The form includes multipage reports, and dynamic content, and retains automatic answers to meet the key requirements.

Outcomes

Empowered Business Users with Enterprise-Grade Business Sites

SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition enables business users to boost productivity and engagement by creating digital workplace solutions. This platform provides a central entry point for accessing business processes, applications, communication, and information from any device.

Business Value:

  • Improve productivity by accessing apps, processes, and information all in one place.
  • Experience a seamlessly integrated environment across SAP and third-party apps to accommodate collaboration tools and integrated information
  • With SAP Build Work Zone as a collaboration platform, users will have all the resources needed to make informed decisions faster
  • Say goodbye to clunky workflows, and hello to a world of efficient business apps, reports, and information that promote collaboration and drive success

Key Milestones:

  • Project Duration: < 4 months
  • Unified and Intelligent work experience by providing central access to apps, tasks, notifications, information, and communication.              
  • Optimize Business Processes with employees, partners, customers & suppliers.
  • Reduce TCO by leveraging existing investments in the SAP Business Technology Platform and harmonization of existing systems and repositories.
  • Integrated collaboration capabilities that can be extended to other business processes
  • Seamless SAP and third-party apps integration
  • Establish Central Inbox

Following are some of the business KPIs:

Contact persons:

SAP MaxAttention CoE: Kiran Kola, Rohit Dwivedi 

SAP Product Management: Florian Buech

entry.title: See how SAP Enterprise Support helps customers use SAP Build to empower their digital transformation
entry.content:
SAP Build is a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. It is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) from SAP that helps accelerate development and automation. SAP Build is an enabler for everyone no matter the skill level to create and enhance enterprise applications, process automations, and business sites, all with drag-and-drop simplicity. 

Consisting of three components SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone, it empowers more users to develop irrespective of their skill level thereby lessening the burden on professional developers.

SAP Build Code is a comprehensive application development environment tailored for developers working with Java and JavaScript. The turnkey solution combines runtime and design time capabilities with built-in enterprise solution integrations, DevOps, and Application Lifecycle Management. Joule, the AI copilot that understands your business, is available to assist with code generation.

In this blog, we are elaborating the assets which are available to provide foundational Support to customers as part of their SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition subscription to use the SAP Build portfolio to empower their digital transformation. 

With SAP Enterprise Support, customers can:

  • Receive guidance and realize value through AI-powered services, best practice content, remote quality checks, and assessment services. 
  • Empower your team to gain knowledge and skills and collaborate with experts for answers to support-related questions.
  • Transform your end-to-end landscape with application lifecycle management solutions to deploy, operate, and optimize cloud and hybrid business solutions.

If you want to just gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio we have provided this SAP Build Big Picture session. In this e-learning, you will gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio including SAP Build Code. It discusses the importance of the Clean Core concept and explore the low code/no code tools available for building new applications. Additionally, a high-level overview of SAP Build Code application development, assisted by Joule, is provided. For more detail, we have provided all the overview sessions for the SAP Build portfolio in the learning program Learn to develop using low-code through SAP Build provided through the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map program.

Below we have listed the SAP Build components and the corresponding support provided to customers to get started:

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation powers the automation of workflows and tasks with comprehensive workflow management, robotic process automation, and AI capabilities.  

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Process Automation. This includes an introductory session which has a demo the tool in action. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Process Automation. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Process Automation. In the first step, we give an overview of process automation in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and customers learn how to create workflows, decision management, and task automation with powerful yet intuitive no-code capabilities through the configuration of a specific business process to approve Sales Orders automatically. Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is an evolution of SAP AppGyver and it enables users to build web and mobile enterprise applications.  With hundreds of templates and pre-built business logic components, you can jumpstart your application projects and reduce development time. 

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Apps. This includes an introductory session. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Apps. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Apps. In the first step, we give an overview of application development in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Apps and customers learn how to create mobile and web applications, connect to SAP systems, and deploy and run on SAP Business Technology Platform.​ Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone, brings the capabilities of SAP Launchpad and SAP Work Zone into one solution, facilitating the rapid creation of engaging and personalized business sites. It provides centralized access to business application information on any device.

To give an overview on SAP Build Work Zone, we have created the e-learning Explore the power of SAP Build Work Zone - SAP BTP Adoption Tutorial which provides a comprehensive Central Entry Point Digital experience. It encompasses an introduction to essential tools such as SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center, and SAP Mobile Start. Key topics are discussed including the capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, both standard and advanced editions and Identity and Access management through the SAP Cloud Identity Service. Also, we take a deep dive into the One Inbox suite quality of the SAP Task Center. and SAP Mobile Start as your native mobile entry point.

To provide further information for SAP Build Work Zone, we have created additional e-learnings. Firstly, we give an overview to integrate SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and SAP Mobile Start. We walk you through the configuration process for the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition ensuring you gain a comprehensive understanding of its setup and functionalities. Also, you will gain proficiency in integrating SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Also, we have provided an e-learning for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition Deep Dive to effectively configure, deploy, and manage SAP Build Work Zone environments, leveraging its capabilities to enhance collaboration and productivity within your organization. The content shows how to set up SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition with integration of IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) for user access, the process of creating a workspace and adding content such as How-to documents and technical support access, developing a UI Card in SAP Business Application Studio and deploying it to the workspace and integrating the Launchpad service and adding exposed content from an SAP S/4HANA system.

SAP Build Code

Apart from the SAP Build Big Picture session which provides an overview to SAP Build Code, we are piloting a new Expert Guided Implementation service on Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code. The purpose of this service is to empower customers with the knowledge of CAP development in SAP HANA Cloud and provide customers with the knowledge to develop and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio using SAP Build Code and the SAP AI co-pilot Joule. To find out more about this pilot through SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council, please reach out directly.

The e-learning Clean Core with SAP Business Technology Platform offers valuable insights to understand how SAP Business Technology Platform can help cultivate a cleaner and more sustainable core for SAP systems. It offers insights into how SAP BTP can support the fundamental pillars of data, integration, and extensibility. It explores the topic of extensibility in-depth and how the various components of SAP Business Technology Platform support the building of extensions in line with clean core principles.

For customers, if you have issues accessing the above links, there may be an authorizations issue. Please note a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required for most of the above assets. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

For internal colleagues, if you cannot access the above links, please use the following link, click on “Access” and use the above direct link once access has been granted.

We hope that this range of content and assets will support you in your digital transformation. For any queries in relation to any of the above assets, please reach out to us directly.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

SAP BTP Lead Architect – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: SAP Jam Collaboration sunset and which products does SAP offer as alternative solutions?
entry.content:
You are an SAP customer, who is still running SAP Jam Collaboration solutions? Then this blog post is relevant for you.

SAP Jam Collaboration was SAP’s enterprise collaboration platform and modern intranet solution for a long time. Then SAP made the strategic decision to exit the standalone collaboration area and focus on the Digital Workplace Experience market with products such as SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. As a result, SAP announced already in 2021 the maintenance mode and planned sunset of SAP Jam Collaboration for beginning of 2027. This includes all SAP Jam Collaboration editions: Basic, Advanced, Advanced Plus, and Enterprise.

What is the status of SAP Jam Collaboration in 2024?

  • All SAP Jam Collaboration editions are now in maintenance mode and were removed from the SAP pricelist in 2021.
  • While no new enhancements will be made, they continue to be fully supported in accordance with the customers’ cloud contract. It is planned to sunset SAP Jam in January 2027.
  • SAP Jam Collaboration is also not available for purchase for new customers, existing customers can continue to use their SAP Jam Collaboration products for the duration of their contract.
  • If you are currently licensed for SAP Jam Collaboration Advanced, Advanced Plus or Enterprise Editions, you can renew these subscriptions provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.
  • If you have benefited from SAP Jam Collaboration Basic Edition, that was provided complimentary to your SAP SuccessFactors subscription, you are welcome to continue to use this until your contract end date, provided that the end date of the contract does not exceed December 2026.

Note: customers will not be able to use SAP Jam Collaboration after end of life of the solution, and all systems would be removed permanently after this date. Therefore it is very important for customers to plan a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to the alternative products before December 2026 or by the end of their cloud contract.

Is there a legal successor for SAP Jam Collaboration? What is the recommended go-to product for SAP customers ?

There is no legal successor product for SAP Jam Collaboration editions. SAP has released alternative products with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone – which provide many features known from SAP Jam Collaboration editions, but also offer a broad range of additional capabilities and innovative features. With these new products, various use cases like engaging employee experience platforms, modern intranets or integrated digital workplace scenarios can be implemented. 

Why is SAP investing into a unified workplace experience that increases engagement and productivity of end users?

When asking employees about the primary operational challenge that they encounter when trying to accomplish their day-to-day work activities, you will often get the answer that it is too hard for them find the data that they need, that they need too much time by switching between applications and finding the right applications to accomplish the tasks. The work experience is fragmented between multiple tools, tons of systems, repositories and applications. If we look at a typical SAP customer system landscape, the end users often need to access multiple SAP systems cloud and on-premise with multiple user experiences via desktop and mobile devices. SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Successfactors Work Zone offer a solution to this problem. They enable companies, IT departments and employees to improve this situation by bringing it all together and making it easier to consume. Both products deliver consistent and engaging user experiences across all channels, business processes, and applications.

What is SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP Build Work Zone empowers IT professionals and line of business experts to easily build and design engaging business sites for employees, partners and customers with intuitive drag-and-drop functionality. It connects to all systems (SAP and non-SAP) by creating a single access point to applications, processes, information and data on any device, from desktop or mobile, and it maximize team productivity by enabling guided experiences and knowledge sharing.

It is available in two editions:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, provides a central access point to multiple SAP cloud & on-premise systems, integrating apps and providing customizations and extensions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, provides advanced features for content management, web content, interactive and engaging workspaces, and integration with third party solutions. This image shows an example of a homepage created with the advanced edition:

For a migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, the advanced edition is the correct migration path, as workspaces - the evolution of Jam groups - are only available in the advanced edition. 

See also: 

  • SAP Build Work Zone on SAP Community
  • SAP Build Work Zone on sap.com
  • FAQs

What is SAP Successfactors Work Zone and what is the difference to SAP Build Work Zone?

SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a comprehensive digital workplace solution offering a centralized hub for all work-related needs. From accessing information and completing tasks to learning, collaborating, and seeking support, SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone provides a single entry point for employees to easily navigate their work environment. SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone is a dedicated solution bundle with user-based subscription model. The package includes SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, plus several SAP BTP services (such as SAP Business Application Studio, Custom Domain). It includes the same feature set as the regular SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

All business content packages (such as HR) with pre-built UI Integration Cards, workflows (guided experiences) and workspace templates are also available in both commercial options: SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as well as SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. More information: Commercial Models and Metering.

See also:
What is SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone? 
SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone on sap.com

Will there be tools available to support migration from SAP Jam Collaboration to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

There are different manual and API-based options to export content, details can be found here:

  • How to export a complete SAP Jam group into a zip file
  • SAP Jam - How to backup or transfer content from one instance to another

These options will remain available until the end of customer contract for SAP Jam or at the latest until the final sunset date which is planned for January 2027.

Apart from this and in case customers decide to purchase and implement either SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, an out-of-the-box migration and upgrade path is available. Details can be found in respective onboarding guides for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. During the onboarding process a configuration wizard is available, which supports the creation of a service instance based on an existing SAP Jam tenant:

Why should customers migrate to SAP Build Work Zone?

(Note that this section is also relevant for SAP Successfactors Work Zone as it includes the same feature set as SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.)

Features and Innovation
SAP Build Work Zone and especially the advanced edition will give you a lot of innovative features, which go far beyond the feature range that you know from SAP Jam Collaboration:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition includes most capabilities of SAP Jam Enterprise edition (and hence implicitly from the lower editions). It also provides many additional features not available in SAP Jam, like menu builder, UI Integration cards and guided experiences.
  • While admin areas and homepages are still part of the menu, a more flexible role-based menu builder with workpages is available. Workpages replace home & overview pages combining pre-built widgets (from SAP Jam) & Build Work Zone specific ability to add applications & UI Integration Cards.
  • Workspaces as part of SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition represent the evolution of SAP Jam groups with expanded scope and capabilities. Workspace offer simplified access control while optionally allowing access to external users. Unlike groups, workspaces can also offer a true read-only experience hiding all collaborative elements/features.
  • As you know from SAP Jam, SAP Build Work Zone also supports both uploaded (Office, PDF, Video, etc.) as well as natively created (blogs, wikis, workpages, etc.) content.
  • SAP Build Work Zone provides a user profile with profile-level content, feeds & settings and always provisioned using SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • Joule integration is also planned for SAP Build Work Zone in 2024.  

Smooth migration path
Another motivation to start a migration project is the fact that the migration path from SAP Jam Collaboration to Work Zone is technically a smooth transition supported fully by tools. Also SAP IT went through this transition experience in 2022 with a large project covering 145 TB of data, 15 million documents and +150.000 Jam groups converted to workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. November 25, 2022 was the date when SAP employees were provided a new digital collaborative workplace with SAP Build Work Zone replacing SAP Jam.
The cutover to SAP Build Work Zone involved remarkably little disruption for users. The "pure" technical migration took place only over one weekend. On Fridays, users still worked with SAP Jam groups. On Monday, they logged in and had SAP Build Work Zone workspaces in place, including all the content. Straight after the migration, several thousand groups (now: workspaces) were operating smoothly, letting employees complete tasks and interact with customers, partners, and other stakeholders.

Check out this video and learn from the SAP IT team how they empowered SAP employees with a modern digital workplace. You can also learn from the SAP migration project lead, Eduardo Antunes da Cunha, about his experiences with the technical migration to SAP Build Work Zone:

Watch the video!

What are the next steps?

  • Evaluate your use cases within SAP Jam Collaboration to determine your requirements.
  • Collaborate with your account team to discuss SAP's alternative solutions to meet your requirements and business needs.
  • Plan for your migration from SAP Jam Collaboration before December 2026 or by the end of your cloud contract (whichever is the earliest date). Do not miss the date: the migration support tools require that SAP Jam Collaboration is still available at customers’ site to support a migration to SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone.
  • If you would like to explore the extended capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, please reach out to your account team to strategize on your options.

entry.title: Understand CSRF handling in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
What is CSRF?

CSRF stands for Cross-Site Request Forgery. It is a type of malicious attack that tricks the victim into submitting a malicious request. It uses the identity and privileges of the victim to perform an undesired function on their behalf. For instance, this could involve changing an email address or password, or making a purchase. The attack can cause damage even if the exploited action is normally harmless because it's performed without the user's consent or knowledge.

What is the defense mechanism?

CSRF token is a security measure used in web applications to protect against CSRF attacks.

It is a unique, random value associated with a user's session and is typically embedded within a form or URL to ensure that the user is intentionally submitting the request. This token is checked by the server to verify the source of the request, preventing malicious parties from tricking a user's browser into making a request on their behalf.

This is usually taken care of by the browser from where the app is launched and accesses APIs.

CSRF tokens are mainly used for communication with the server. Before an app is allowed to execute a modify operation through an HTTP request such as POST, PUT, DELETE etc., a CSRF token is generated by the server to ensure the security of the operation.

Here is a brief process of how it is used:

  1. First, the app sends a GET request to fetch a CSRF token from the server.
  2. During the GET request, the server generates a new CSRF token and sends it back in the response headers (usually under "x-csrf-token").
  3. The app then uses this token in the header of every modification request.
  4. The server validates the CSRF token in every modifying request. If it's valid, the operation proceeds.
  5. If it's not valid or missing, the server returns a 403 (Forbidden) status, and the operation does not proceed.

In this way, the CSRF token ensures that every operation modifying data is secure, authenticated, and originated from a trusted source. It protects against CSRF attacks where an attacker might trick a victim into executing actions of the attacker's choosing.

Why do we need additional CSRF handling for SAP Build Apps?

To ensure application security, it is essential to understand and manage CSRF token use effectively, as it is crucial in preventing unwanted actions from being performed.

In SAP Build Apps because of the current architecture, extra CSRF token handling is required sometimes for end-user deployed apps with a back-end connection accessing an API indirectly (via a destination such as S/4 HANA) wherein the request to the API goes through via managed app-router.

To be more precise, all installed destinations are CSRF protected by managed app-router, by default. However, if a backend behind a destination has its own CSRF protection, you need to tell SAP Build Apps to switch off managed app-router’s CSRF protection for that specific destination.

This is because when your app is fetching the CSRF token, it will end up getting the CSRF token from managed app-router and then it will send it to backend and fail because the backend doesn’t recognize that token.

What is the consequence of not handling the CSRF in SAP Build Apps?

In this case the symptom would be that the deployed build apps fail with a 403 (forbidden) error as illustrated in one example below.

Disclaimer: As of today it is not possible to show this failure early in the Preview mode as the request handling here (and in the design time) is done via destination-proxy. So in general, no additional CSRF handling is required for design-time and preview mode.

How to overcome this issue?

To overcome this issue, we need to extend your destination (only for some backends which has it's own CSRF protection (such as S/4 Hana backends)) with the tag below to tell the Build Apps to switch off the managed app-router’s CSRF protection for that specific destination.

 

BuildApps.RequiresCsrf = true

 

This way, when your app is fetching the CSRF token, managed app-router won’t interfere and you will get the CSRF token from backend and then when you send it to backend it will work (because the backend will recognize that token).

NOTE: If the SAP Build apps is already built and deployed then we need to also rebuild and redeploy the application for this change in the destination to take effect.

Additional Reading

Refer to the SAP Systems | SAP Help Portal under the 'Adding Data Endpoints' section.

entry.title: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In this blog, I would like to share my experience of Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP

Below, I'll guide you through a simple step-by-step process to Deploy App to SAP Build Work Zone

Overview:

SAP Build Work Zone, standard enables organizations to establish a unified point access to SAP (e.g. SAP S/4HANA), custom-built, and third-party applications and extensions, both on the cloud and on premise 

SAP Build Work Zone is a Service which is provided in SAP BTP 

In SAP Build Zone we can create a site, this site is somewhat similar to Fiori Launchpad which is named as SAP Build Work Zone in SAP BTP. 

 

How To Deploy Application to SAP Build Work Zone

Navigate to your BTP Sub account  

  • Go to Service -> Service Marketplace  
  • Here we will select those services which I want to create instance of  
  • First, we must subscribe SAP BAS Build Work Zone service, So Search for SAP Build Work Zone and click on Create

 

Here we need to provide basic Information for our instance or subscription, for that click on plan dropdown. There we will find two plans 

  • Standard Subscription  
  •  Standard Instance  

Select standard subscription and click on create  

Then click on view subscription 

 

After clicking on view subscription, we can see status as subscribed in Instances and Subscription section 

Then click on SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition 

 

After clicking on SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and we will get access denied/authorization error

 

You have to assign  Launchpad_Admin role collection, so you don’t get an error accessing the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition site  

So go to Security -> users and give required permission in BTP 

 

Click on Assign role collection and Assign Launchpad_Admin , after that logout and login. 

Note: - when you have provided a new role, it is required to logout and login again so that particular role gets activated.

 

 

Now go to Services -> Instances and Subscriptions and click on SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition 

There you can see a site manager or site directory page, this is the place where we can create site 

 

Then click on Create Site button and give Site Name and create 

 

After creating site we can able to default settings page, here we will not change anything we will keep everything as it is. This will create a sample site 

 

 here we can see that Fiori Sample Site is created 

Next, we need to create UI5 app while creating we need to give  

  • Select Add deployment configuration as yes  
  • Select Add FLP configuration as yes 

 

Choose target as Cloud Foundry  

And select destination if don’t have destination keep it as none

This is the configuration which we will see, or this is going to what you are tile of SAP site look like  

  • Give Semantic object (ex: - simplehello) 
  • Select Action as Show 
  • Give Title (ex:  - Simple App 1) 

To deploy an application, right click on mta.yaml and click on Build MTA Project. It will build our entire project, and it will create mta_archives folder, under folder we can see mtar file 

 

 

Once build is done right click on mtar file and click on Deploy MTA Archies  

 

In BAS we have to login Cloud Foundry before any build  

So, provide username and password 

 

 

Then it will ask you to select Cloud Foundry Organization and Cloud Foundry Space 

 

Now you can see three services is created under Services -> instances and Subscriptions

  • Destination - service 
  • Html5-srv 
  • Xsuapp-srv 

 

Go to Site manager -> channel Manager and refresh it under Action section 

(Services -> Instances and Subscriptions and click on SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition) it will go to Site Manager

After refreshing we will have access to the Newly deployed Application 

Then click on content explorer button from there click on HTML5 apps 

 

 

After that Select check box and click on add button 

Next create group and provide a group name (ex: - Simple Group 1) and click on Save

After clicking on save that group will be having app 

 

Now we have to assign role to see the application  

Currently the application will not be visible to you unless you have the everyone role assign to it  

So, navigate to everyone role and click on edit, and assign application to everyone role by clicking on Assignment status and save it 

 

Now the application is associated with everyone role  

Now everyone role is assign to all the users in SAP BTP that you can see under Translate section 

 

Then go to site Directory and click on go to site icon  

 

Then we can see the group and app.

  

Thanks and Regards,

Hari Kumar Talari

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q3 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q3 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance and a new product page we've made significant improvements to the user experience:

  1. New Side Menu for improved page navigation
  2. New UI Elements for additional context
  3. New Search Feature 

Read more about the details of these enhancements in this blog post.

This section details the new UX features added to the release navigator for SAP BTP.

New Side Menu for Easier Navigation

We've implemented a new side menu on every page of the release navigator to provide a more intuitive user experience. This menu allows easy navigation to the four new main pages of the release navigator:

  1. Product Specific
  2. General
  3. Get Assistance
  4. User Feedback

Fig 1. The side menu on all new pages

As a result, some content previously found on the Home page has been rearranged. Let’s walk through what you can find on each of the new pages.

Product Specific Page

This page serves as the starting point for the release navigator and corresponds to the former Home page. It contains the core of the release navigator: curated release information for specific products and services of SAP BTP as well as information on release schedules.

Fig 2. The Product Specific page, formerly known as the Home page

At the top of the Product Specific Page you can find a message tile with helpful information and a Release Calendar. The calendar includes release dates for SAP BTP as a whole, links to product-specific release schedules and dates for upcoming release navigator updates.

Below you can find the section Products. The cards in this section are sorted alphabetically and each one takes you to a dedicated page with in-depth information and curated resources on the 27 SAP BTP products and services covered.

Product Page Navigation

Fig 3. Example of a dedicated product page

Once you are done exploring the resources on a product page and want to move on to the next product or service, you can either click on Product Specific on the side-menu or on the back arrow on the left of the page title to get back to the Product Specific page and navigate to other pages.

General Page

On this page you can find General Resources that are helpful across all SAP BTP products and services. If you can't find a certain product or service on the Product Specific page, we recommend you check out the resources on this page (and also feel free to let us know in our survey).

Besides the General resources, there are two more sections on this page:

  • Additional Resources contains non-release specific but nevertheless important resources one should be aware of.
  • Release Navigators by SAP Enterprise Support provide direct links to all currently available release navigators for other solution areas.

Fig 4. The General page

Get Assistance Page

This page contains resources to help you use the release navigator and to improve your experience. You can access it from all pages in the release navigator by either clicking on Get Assistance in the side-menu or by clicking on the red bar at the very top of the page.

At the top of the page, you can find the following elements:

  • Release Navigator Enablement:
    • SAP Community Blog Posts: These blog posts go into detail about new features that are being added to the release navigator and provide other news.
    • SAP Partner Masterclass: This recorded digital session is only available to SAP partners.
  • Translation Feature: Allows you to translate the release navigator (not the pages linked to) into 40 languages. The translation process takes a few moments and may impact layout and design of the navigator pages. Click on this card to sign in to the authenticated version with Universal ID or an S-user account to use this feature.
  • User Feedback: Click here to provide feedback in a quick 3 question survey.

Below you can find the Navigation Tips, detailed instructions on how to use the release navigator, similar to the blog post you are reading now.

Fig 5. The Get Assistance page

User Feedback Page

We've embedded a feedback survey on this page with three short questions to gather feedback and suggestions for improvement.

  • Your relationship to SAP.
  • Whether you found the release navigator helpful.
  • An open text field that allows you to let us know anything you liked, what you would like to see added, or what you think could be improved.

We strive to always improve the release navigator, so we really appreciate any feedback you submit.

Fig 6. The User Feedback page

Helpful UI Elements

We added two new types of UI elements that you will find throughout the pages of the navigator.

Lightbulb icon

Many pages come with additional information to guide you through the cards available on that page and provide more context on when to use which resource.

If you see a lightbulb icon next to a header, you can hover over it with your cursor to open a text bubble with additional information about that page.

The bubble can be moved around by clicking and holding your cursor and can be closed by clicking the  on the top right.Fig 7. Demo of the lightbulb icon text bubble

On product pages, the information displayed in this bubble corresponds to the information formerly found on Release Guidance tiles.

About this Card

Some resource cards come with additional tips and contextual information.

If you see three dots on the bottom right of a card, you can open a tip with additional information about that particular card. To open the link of that card, you need to close this tip by clicking the three dots button again.

Fig 8. Demo on the About this Card tips

Due to the high demand for dedicated pages for products and services, the number of pages to navigate has grown significantly since launch of the release navigator one year ago and we aim to increase the number of pages even further in future updates. More pages mean more content but also more cards to browse, which can increase the time it takes to find what you’re looking for.

Our goal is for you to find what you need as quickly as possible. That’s why we implemented a new Search Feature, which allows you to search for keywords and find all pages associated with this topic, product or service name. Click on the  Search icon at the shell bar and a pop-up will open. Once you enter key words, you will see which pages are associated to this term and you can jump directly to them. The search feature is optimized for product and service names.

Fig 9. Short demo of the Search function.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add a new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery. This page contains links to the release schedule, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New Viewer in the SAP Help Portal and more. Check it out.

Fig 10. The new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery.

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 27 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

Last update, we announced the new SAP Note Consolidated Release Schedules for SAP BTP and the corresponding initiative of communicating the release schedules of SAP BTP products and services.

We are happy to report that the number of products and services who are communicating their release schedules has further increased to more than 50 now thanks to close collaboration with various SAP BTP teams.

Check the schedules here and from the Release Calendar section of the navigator.

Tip: Did you know you can mark notes as a favorite? If you do so, you will be notified via email whenever the note is being updated. This way you can stay on top of new release schedule communication, like new dates or skipped waves. We recommend you mark all those notes as a favorite that you are interested in receiving updates for.

Fig 11. Where to click to mark a note as a favorite and receive notifications once it gets updated.

Please note: To access SAP Notes you need to log in to SAP for Me using your Universal ID or S-user ID. Read more about the required authorizations here.

Next updates are scheduled on a quarterly basis in alignment with the Harmonized Release Calendar for SAP Cloud Products.*Disclaimer: The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Explore SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right across the corner and the spotlight shines brighter than ever on SAP Build—a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. Whether you're an experienced developer or just beginning your digital journey, SAP Build is here to provide business you with the tools needed to simplify app creation, automate processes, and design digital workspaces.

This blog will focus on SAP Build’s low-code offerings at TechEd 2024, highlighting key sessions that showcase how low-code can empower you to drive innovation and transformation within your organization.

SAP TechEd Experience 2024 

SAP TechEd 2024 kicks off with an exciting, free virtual event that’s open to everyone, offering a truly engaging experience no matter where you are. It is a great chance to connect with industry experts, developers, and IT leaders to discover innovative strategies that will elevate your career and drive your company’s success.

   📅 Save the date: October 8–9, 2024 

   🤝 Interactive, accessible, and virtual event

   🚀 Register now! 

Following this, SAP TechEd on Tour will bring deep dive, in-person learning experience to various locations worldwide. 

 

Highlight: SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd virtual 2024 

   🔷 AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

Speakers: Thomas Volmering, Björn Schulz, Alexander Rother 

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

Explore a fresh approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs.

   🔷 AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Speakers: Florian Buech, Karthik Thirunavukkarasan

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 11:00 AM - 11:25 AM CEST

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

   🔷 AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Speakers: Tim Back, Anne Keller

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

   🔷 AD108 | Enhance your business efficiency with SAP Build

Speakers: Yumnah Abbasi, Sruthi Chiramel

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:30 PM - 8:55 PM CEST

Find out how customers extend their processes with SAP Build low-code solutions across HR, procurement, supply chain, finance, and sustainability applications. Learn how you can use prebuilt content in SAP Build to accelerate and benefit from the latest features, such as guided experiences.

   🔷 AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP

Speakers: Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, Palak Garg

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 1:00 PM - 1:25 PM CEST

Explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together. Drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. Extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and find out how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modelling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale in heterogenous landscapes.

   🔷 AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build

Speakers: Rupam Bhattacharya, Marc Huber

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and AI features in SAP build that improve productivity of its users. Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience Gen AI powered fusion development firsthand in SAP Build. Learn how to automate processes and work flow productively with AI capabilities. Get a glimpse of what’s new and latest in AI features within SAP Build.

   🔷 AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule (Jump-Start session) 

Speakers: Eric Solberg, Patrick Schad

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST

Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

   🔷 AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Speakers: Maria Kondratyeva, Miguel Angel Meza Martinez

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the powerful synergy between Joule technology and SAP Build solutions that facilitates a smooth extension-building process. Learn how to enhance AI-powered capabilities in Joule with custom-developed enterprise skills tailored to your company's specific needs. By integrating with both SAP and third-party applications, Joule can instantly apply its newfound knowledge within SAP systems. 

----------------------------------------

In addition to SAP Build highlights, SAP TechEd virtual offers a wide range of sessions, organized into six content tracks. These tracks cover the latest SAP technologies, products, and solutions, ensuring that you can dive deep into the topics most relevant to your role and industry.

   🔍 Browse the full session catalog!

In the Application Development and Automation track, you can dive into the latest innovations in SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Code, SAPUI5, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and more. The track offers practical demos and insights from our top speakers and product experts. 

   👩‍💻 Check out this blogpost for further details!

----------------------------------------

I hope this sneak peek has sparked your excitement to be part of SAP TechEd 2024 and explore all the latest updates around SAP Build and beyond. 

We can’t wait to see you there – Happy TechEd! 😊

entry.title: Enhancing Business Efficiency with SAP Build: Work Zone, Apps, and Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides a robust suite of tools to help organizations build, extend, and enhance their digital capabilities. Among these tools are SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Each tool serves a unique purpose, from creating digital workplaces to developing custom applications and automating processes. This blog introduces these tools, outlines their key features, and provides examples to help you determine when to use each.

Intended Audience

This blog is intended for SAP developers, IT professionals, business process owners, and decision-makers looking to optimize digital experiences, streamline workflows, and accelerate digital transformation. Whether you are a developer seeking low-code solutions, an IT manager focusing on automation, or a business leader aiming to enhance employee productivity, this blog will help you understand how SAP Build tools can drive value for your organization.

Understanding the SAP Build Umbrella

SAP Build is a unified suite within SAP BTP that encompasses SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Together, these tools empower both business users and developers to design intuitive user interfaces, create custom applications, and automate complex workflows, all without the need for extensive coding skills.

  • SAP Build Work Zone focuses on creating a personalized digital workspace, where employees can access all their tools and information in one place.
  • SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that enables rapid development of custom applications.
  • SAP Build Process Automation allows users to automate repetitive tasks and streamline business processes efficiently.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone enables organizations to create a unified digital workspace that enhances employee experience by integrating multiple applications, data sources, and services into a single, customizable portal. It is ideal for companies aiming to improve employee productivity and engagement by providing a personalized and consolidated workspace.

  • Key Features:
  • Unified access to SAP and third-party apps
  • Customizable user interfaces with drag-and-drop functionality
  • Pre-built templates for quick deployment
  • Role-based content delivery

Example Use Case: Create a central portal for HR that consolidates employee self-service tools, payroll information, training resources, and internal communication platforms.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that allows users to rapidly develop and deploy custom applications. Designed for both business users and developers, it offers a visual development environment to build responsive applications that can connect to SAP and non-SAP data sources.

  • Key Features:
  • Low-code/no-code application development
  • Drag-and-drop interface for rapid prototyping
  • Pre-built connectors for easy integration with various data sources
  • Flexibility to customize app logic and design

Example Use Case: Develop a mobile application for field service agents to access real-time inventory data, log service requests, and update task statuses without needing extensive coding knowledge.

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation allows organizations to automate repetitive, manual tasks and streamline business processes using SAP’s AI and machine learning capabilities. This tool is perfect for companies looking to reduce errors, improve efficiency, and lower operational costs.

  • Key Features:
  • Visual workflow design and automation
  • Integration with SAP and third-party systems
  • Pre-built bot templates and AI services
  • Monitoring and analytics for process optimization

Example Use Case: Automate the end-to-end invoice processing workflow, from data extraction to approval, reducing manual intervention and accelerating processing times.

Comparison of SAP Build Tools

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

SAP BTP offers a powerful set of tools — SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation — to help organizations enhance employee experiences, rapidly develop applications, and automate processes. Start by identifying key areas where these tools can provide the most value, and consider initiating a small pilot project to explore their potential benefits. In the next blog, we’ll discuss advanced integration techniques and best practices for maximizing your SAP BTP investments.

entry.title: SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition. SAML configuration.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In this blog, I will try to clarify the architecture of SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition (SAP WZAE) with a focus on the SAML authentication process. Many existing guides provide step-by-step instructions, but often lack explanation of the key concepts behind the process. This blog will aim to fill that gap and provide clarity on the roles of IAS, IPS, SAML, and the relationship between SAP WZAE and DWS. Why SAML is emphasized - the SAML setup and understanding are a key to successfully install SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition. 

Without a deeper understanding of key concepts such as IAS, IPS, tenants and subaccounts in BTP, SAML, SAP WorkZone app (frontend), SAP DWS (backend), JAM (sometimes SAP SuccessFactors and the SAP Cloud Foundry environment) — the process of installing and configuring the SAP WorkZone ecosystem can feel random and unnecessarily challenging

The following description provides the list of main components that are required for authentication in SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition application:

  • IAS (Identity Authentication Service): A cloud-based service that handles authentication for users in SAP systems, enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) and secure user access using standards like SAML.

  • IPS (Identity Provisioning Service): A service for automating user provisioning, synchronizing users, roles, and permissions between different identity providers and target systems.

  • SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language): A protocol used for Single Sign-On (SSO) that allows identity providers (IdP) to authenticate users and authorize access to service providers (SP) securely.

  • SAP WorkZone App (Frontend): The user interface of SAP WorkZone, where users interact with applications, content, and workflows to manage their work and access business applications.

  • SAP DWS (Digital Workplace Service): The backend component of SAP WorkZone that manages user data, workspace configurations, and content through APIs, providing administrative functions and integration capabilities.

Please note, this guide will not provide another step-by-step installation guide for SAP WZAE. Instead, it aims to complement the existing blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072, which already provides detailed instructions on setting up SAP WZAE (in my opinion a few steps are not needed, but we will cover it later).

SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition (WZAE) and BTP

In simple terms, the BTP platform is gradually replacing SAP NetWeaver as the core platform for modern SAP applications. Just like SAP NetWeaver, BTP's kernel should be supported by the SAP Basis or another technical department in my view, as it involves many configurations and settings typically managed by SAP Basis teams in the NetWeaver environment.

To successfully configure and maintain a reliable and well-documented SAP WorkZone application or any other application in BTP, it's crucial to understand the architecture of SAP BTP, like we used to work with the architecture of classic SAP NetWeaver that includes few Application Servers, Database (single or cluster), and Web Dispatcher (optional), SSO and SAML configuration! The deeper understanding of the BTP backend ensures that the application setup is both effective and stable.

My recommendation for project associated with BTP - the team must have a consultant with technical background (SAP Platform support), otherwise you are in the risk to start the ping pong with SAP Support during implementation and finally will need to attract externals to finish setup.

Example of the SAP BTP Environment

This is a very basic diagram illustrating the different levels of the BTP environment. The key on this diagram is SAP Cloud Identity Service. The idea is to show that you don’t need to have the individual instance of SAP CIS inside of each Subaccount. It’s like a CUA system, usually there are two instances for all landscape - one for all production environments and another one for non productions.

When setting up SAP WorkZone, it is crucial for an administrator to have a solid understanding of the SAML protocol and how it is managed (IAS or IdP is installed inside of Cloud Identity Service that can work like standalone or proxy app. for local IdP [for instance MS Active Directory]). Knowing the concepts of the Identity Provider (IdP) and Service Provider (SP) — which are key components of SAML— is vital for ensuring a successful setup.

Key Components of SAP WorkZone

Before switch to technical aspects, it is important to note that SAP Work Zone application contains two key components:

  1. Front End component – SAP Build WorkZone advanced edition (SaaS)
  2. Backend component – Digital Workplace Service (DWS). Particularly, this instance stores user settings and authorization

SAML Configuration for SAP WZAE

Now the SAML, I would like to pay an attention on it because this is a key step in the configuration of SAP WZAE. The rest of steps that you can find in the official guide or blog are not so difficult:

  • official guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/prerequisites
  • blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072

Authentication process in SAP Work Zone:

Authentication Process in SAP Work Zone

Let’s breakdown the above diagram:

  1. To be able to login in the Work Zone, the user must be created in IAS:

1.1 Additionally, the trust relationship must be established between subaccount of SAP WZAE and SAP IAS:

1.2 “Available for User Logon” option must be selected in the configuration of IAS:

1.3 The default identity provider should be disabled to prevent the usage of S-USER for connection:

2. SAP WZAE sends SAML request to IAS during login:

3. IAS sends back the SAML response with details about user:

3.1 Please pay an attention on AttributeStatement area, this is quite important. To provide a proper response the attributes of SAP WZAE application in IAS must be configured properly:

The saml trace can be obtained with SAML-tracer extension that is available in chrome:

Just turn it on before open the login page

4. Here the SAP magic starts - to be able to connect SAP WZAE app it makes the request to DWS. SAP WZAE calls the internal API /dynamic_dest/JAM/v1/single_use_tokens to generate the token and if it is ok, the response contains the location of DWS:

Our experience - we had the issue at this stage, this step likely failed because the subject name attributes were not correctly configured for the WorkZone application in IAS. However, since the URL included the "JAM" prefix, I initially thought we had missed something with the JAM integration, as JAM is mentioned multiple times in the official documentation:

As we later realized, JAM integration is not required, and there is no need for a connection with JAM at all (honestly, I still don’t know how the integration with JAM works, and I don’t intend to find out until this is mandatory for setup). The fact remains that the connection to JAM is not necessary to complete the setup of SAP WorkZone and /dynamic_dest/JAM/v1/single_use_tokens is not associated with JAM although JAM is presented in the URI.

The need to set up a SAML application for DWS is not well explained and can actually complicate the setup and troubleshooting process. Initially, I followed the instructions provided in this blog and created the application in IAS as suggested. However, after further investigation, I ended up deleting this application because I couldn’t find a valid reason to keep it. It didn’t seem to play a necessary role in the overall setup of SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition.

For your reference, the details about DWS can be obtained by using site manager configuration https://<the name of you wz_app>.dt.workzone.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com:

5. In accordance with diagram SAP WorkZone connects to DWS with ticket and here the IPS begins playing. So to that moment everything is good and you are sure you have the user that belongs to the one of the SAP Work Zone group:

But as we found this is not enough. To complete a user setup, IAS data must be replicated to the DWS and the bridge between SAP WZAE DWS and IAS is IPS 😊. In accordance with blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-onboarding-series-step-by-step-guide-to-activate-your-sap-build/ba-p/13555072 you have to create the source and target system in IPS and run the job to replicate the data. It's important, if you want to provide the access for new user, after user creation in IAS the resynch job must be executed in IPS to replicate data in DWS!

Why we need to create the SAML configuration for DWS in IAS I did not find. Finally, I deleted the SAML for DWS because it is not using.

How to find the location of IAS and IPS: https://iamtenants.accounts.cloud.sap/

I hope an explained 6 steps will give you a little more understanding of the backend of SAP Work Zone. A detailed setup guide is not included here for the following reasons:

  1. A detailed and accurate guide has already been provided in the blog by Nagesh Caparthy, which I recommend referring to for step-by-step instructions on setting up SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition.
  2. I hope that SAP will release a comprehensive guide for BTP similar to BC360. Without this, navigating the environment can feel like working blind. Ideally, we could benefit from documentation that is as clear and structured as the Integration & Exception Monitoring - Setup & Configuration guide, which is simple, user-friendly, and effective. For reference, you can find that guide here.
  3. If you encounter issues during the SAP WorkZone setup or with any other application involving SAML, feel free to share your experiences or ask questions in the comments below. I am happy to help!

To conclude, the key to successfully setting up SAP WorkZone Advanced Edition lies in understanding its architecture and the role of SAML for authentication. While existing step-by-step guides are useful, a deeper understanding of concepts like IAS, IPS, and DWS ensures smoother implementation.
